questions.q: Adversarial examples exploit the geometry of neural network decision boundaries — small perturbations in specific directions can cross boundaries while remaining imperceptible to humans.
Which adversarial attack method is considered the strongest first-order attack and is the standard for evaluating adversarial robustness?
options: FGSM (Fast Gradient Sign Method) — a single-step attack
PGD (Projected Gradient Descent) — a multi-step iterated attack

questions.q: The 2017 physical adversarial stop sign experiment demonstrated that adversarial examples only work in digital pipelines and cannot survive real-world printing and lighting conditions.
In the PGD attack formula, the epsilon (ε) parameter controls:
options: The maximum allowed perturbation size (L∞ norm constraint)
The number of gradient descent steps performed

questions.q: Adversarial training — training on adversarial examples — is currently the most effective known defense against adversarial examples.
ML-based security tools like AI antivirus classifiers are immune to adversarial evasion because they use learned features rather than fixed signatures.
In the Cylance AI bypass, what technique was used to evade the ML antivirus engine?
options: Encrypting the malware payload with a novel cipher algorithm
Appending benign strings from a video game executable to shift the feature vector

questions.q: Functional evasion of an ML malware detector means the modified malware sample both evades detection AND preserves its malicious functionality.
The GAMMA framework applies what technique to automatically evade ML malware detectors?
options: Gradient descent over a differentiable malware feature space
Brute-force mutation testing across all possible byte sequences

questions.q: Which defensive approach makes ML malware detectors harder to evade by making their features unpredictable to the attacker?
options: Feature randomization — varying the features used at inference time
Increasing the training dataset size with more malware samples

questions.q: A typical production ML pipeline has only one attack surface: the model serving endpoint.
CI/CD configuration files (like GitHub Actions workflows) can reveal ML pipeline architecture including data storage locations, model registries, and deployment infrastructure.
In the Microsoft Tay attack, what pipeline vulnerability did attackers discover and exploit?
options: An unpatched SQL injection in the Tay admin portal
A 'repeat after me' feature that fed user input directly into the model's training pipeline without filtering

questions.q: If a model registry like MLflow has no authentication on the internal network, what high-impact attack becomes trivial for an attacker with internal network access?
options: Replacing the production model with a backdoored version
Increasing the model's inference latency via resource exhaustion

questions.q: An ML pipeline's retraining schedule (e.g., weekly cron) is relevant to an attacker because it determines the poisoning window — the time window during which injected data will be incorporated into the model.
Membership inference attacks exploit the fact that ML models tend to output higher confidence on their training samples than on held-out data.
Which formal defense provides mathematical privacy guarantees that limit what membership inference attacks can reveal?
options: Encrypting the model weights with AES-256 after training

questions.q: Model inversion attacks reconstruct approximate representations of training data using only black-box access to the model's outputs.
In the Facebook membership inference research, what sensitive attribute could be inferred by querying the ad targeting API?
options: Whether a user had joined a sensitive health group (e.g., HIV+ support)
Whether a user's account was created with a real or fake identity

questions.q: Reducing the precision of confidence scores returned by an ML API (e.g., rounding to 2 decimal places) has no effect on membership inference attack accuracy.
Universal adversarial perturbations (UAPs) are image-agnostic — a single perturbation can cause misclassification on most inputs simultaneously.
A backdoored model (BadNets) will typically fail standard accuracy tests because the backdoor degrades its performance on clean inputs.
What makes clean-label backdoor attacks particularly difficult to detect?
options: They require insider access to the target's training infrastructure
The poisoned training samples appear correctly labeled to human reviewers

questions.q: Which tool uses anomaly detection in the feature space to detect backdoor triggers by finding classes whose minimal trigger is unusually small?
options: Neural Cleanse — reverse-engineers triggers for each class and checks for size anomalies
Foolbox — generates adversarial examples to test model robustness

questions.q: UAPs crafted against one model architecture (e.g., ResNet) often transfer and fool other architectures (e.g., VGG, EfficientNet).
Model extraction attacks can produce a functional substitute model without ever accessing the target model's weights or architecture directly.
Gradient inversion attacks break the privacy guarantee of federated learning by:
options: Intercepting encrypted communications between federated clients and the server
Reconstructing original training data from the gradient updates clients share with the server

questions.q: The cost asymmetry in model extraction (stealing vs. training from scratch) makes it economically unviable for small to medium ML models.
In the KnockoffNets extraction of the Acme Sentiment Classifier scenario, what was the cost ratio of stealing vs. building?
options: Over 2,000x cheaper to steal ($240 API+GPU) than to build ($500,000 training)
Roughly equal cost — model extraction requires similar compute to training

questions.q: A key defense against model extraction is to return only hard labels (no confidence scores) from a public ML API.
Data poisoning attacks can target a model's availability (degrade accuracy), integrity (cause targeted misclassification), or fairness (introduce demographic bias).
In the Microsoft Tay attack, the poisoning was delivered through which unfiltered pipeline channel?
options: A compromised admin API that accepted direct model weight updates
A 'repeat after me' feature that incorporated user-provided phrases into the model's training

questions.q: Slow, gradual model corruption via a feedback loop is harder to detect than a sudden catastrophic attack because performance metrics degrade gradually.
Activation clustering detects backdoor triggers by finding:
options: Unusual spikes in GPU utilization during model inference
An anomalous small cluster of samples in the penultimate layer's activation space for a given class

questions.q: In the AcmeBot poisoning scenario, individual poison samples each appeared normal — the malicious bias was only apparent in aggregate across thousands of injected samples.
The complete ATLAS adversarial lifecycle covers 11 tactics, from reconnaissance through to impact on the ML system.
The Microsoft Tay kill chain completed from initial access to complete model shutdown in approximately:
options: 3 weeks at a cost of $50,000 in API queries

questions.q: The NIST AI Risk Management Framework (AI RMF) provides the defensive counterpart to ATLAS. Its four core functions are:
The Clearview AI operation demonstrated that ATLAS-style threats require sophisticated technical exploits and cannot be executed using only publicly available data.
Which ATLAS tactic did the Clearview AI operation primarily use when harvesting 3+ billion facial images from public social media platforms?
options: TA0008 Collection — gathering data at massive scale from public sources
TA0006 Defense Evasion — bypassing platform security controls

questions.q: Which ATT&CK tactic ID covers Reconnaissance?
True or False: Passive reconnaissance (using Shodan, Censys, LinkedIn) sends traffic directly to the target and can trigger IDS alerts.
options: True — passive tools contact the target
False — passive recon queries public indexes, leaving no trace on the target

questions.q: APT29 (Cozy Bear) spent months in reconnaissance before the SolarWinds breach. What was their primary target in that recon phase?
options: SolarWinds' customer list and build pipeline

questions.q: True or False: Stripping metadata from public documents (T1592.002) is a defender action that reduces the attack surface during the recon phase.
options: True — metadata in PDFs can reveal author, software, and company details
False — document metadata is encrypted and cannot be read by attackers

questions.q: Which tool would an attacker use to enumerate subdomains and email addresses from public sources without touching the target?
Resource Development (TA0042) covers activities that happen before the attacker ever contacts a victim. Which technique involves registering lookalike domains and acquiring VPS servers?
True or False: Certificate Transparency logs are a defender tool for detecting newly issued TLS certificates for lookalike domains before they are used in attacks.
options: True — CT logs are public and can be monitored for suspicious cert issuance
False — CT logs are private and only accessible to CAs

questions.q: Lazarus Group's Operation AppleJeus involved building fake cryptocurrency companies and trojanized software. Which ATT&CK technique best describes developing that custom malware?
True or False: A newly registered domain with all WHOIS details hidden and a freshly issued certificate is considered low-risk in threat intelligence analysis.
options: True — new domains are common and should not be flagged automatically
False — new domain + hidden WHOIS + new cert is a high-risk pattern for attacker infrastructure

questions.q: What technique does JA3 TLS fingerprinting help defenders detect during the resource development and C2 operation phases?
options: Identifying C2 frameworks by their distinctive TLS handshake signature
Blocking all outbound TLS traffic from corporate endpoints

questions.q: According to Verizon DBIR data cited in this stage, what percentage of nation-state intrusions use spear phishing as the initial access technique?
True or False: DMARC p=reject configured on a domain prevents attackers from spoofing that domain in phishing emails.
options: True — DMARC p=reject causes receiving mail servers to discard spoofed emails
False — DMARC only logs spoofing attempts but cannot block delivery

questions.q: In the 2016 DNC hack, APT28 gained access to John Podesta's credentials. What made the phishing email convincing?
options: It spoofed a Google security alert and linked to a pixel-perfect login clone
It was sent from a verified Google email address with an embedded malware attachment

questions.q: True or False: Seeing WINWORD.EXE spawning powershell.exe as a child process is expected system administration behavior and should not trigger an EDR alert.
options: True — Word uses PowerShell for macro execution in normal operation
False — this parent-child relationship is a high-fidelity indicator of spear phish exploitation

questions.q: Which email security technique routes links in emails through a proxy that checks destinations against threat intelligence before allowing the user to visit?
PowerShell Script Block Logging (Event ID 4104) captures the decoded content of encoded PowerShell commands. What GPO setting enables it?
options: EnableScriptBlockLogging = 1 under the PowerShell ScriptBlockLogging registry path
EnableTranscription = 1 under the Windows Defender registry path

questions.q: True or False: LOLBins (Living-off-the-land binaries) like certutil.exe, mshta.exe, and regsvr32.exe are unsigned scripts that bypass antivirus.
options: True — LOLBins are custom attacker scripts that evade detection
False — LOLBins are legitimate signed Microsoft binaries abused to execute attacker code

questions.q: NotPetya caused $10B in global damage in 2017. Which execution techniques did it rely on to spread without dropping traditional malware?
options: WMI (T1047) and PsExec (T1569.002) — LOLBin-style legitimate Windows tools
Custom kernel exploits and unsigned driver installation

questions.q: True or False: PowerShell Constrained Language Mode (CLM) combined with AppLocker prevents most PowerShell-based execution techniques by restricting access to .NET types and COM objects.
options: True — CLM + AppLocker blocks most PowerShell attacker techniques
False — CLM only applies to interactive PowerShell sessions, not scripts

questions.q: An attacker uses the flag `-Enc` when running powershell.exe. What does this flag do and how do defenders decode it?
options: It base64-encodes the command to obfuscate it; Event ID 4104 captures the decoded content
It enables encrypted network communication; defenders must capture the traffic with Wireshark

questions.q: Which Windows Event ID signals that a new Scheduled Task has been created — a key persistence detection point?
True or False: APT1 (Comment Crew) maintained access to victim networks for an average of 356 days, relying on layered persistence so that removing one mechanism still left others active.
options: True — APT1 used 20+ persistence techniques simultaneously across victims
False — APT1 was detected quickly; they relied on speed rather than persistence

questions.q: An attacker adds an entry to HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run. Which ATT&CK sub-technique does this represent?
options: T1547.001 — Registry Run Keys / Startup Folder

questions.q: True or False: Finding and removing a single persistence mechanism (e.g., a malicious scheduled task) guarantees that the attacker has been fully evicted from the network.
options: True — once the scheduled task is removed, re-entry is blocked
False — sophisticated attackers layer multiple persistence mechanisms; find them all before declaring clean

questions.q: Which Sysinternals tool provides a comprehensive inventory of all persistence mechanisms on a Windows system, making it the gold standard for manual persistence hunting?
Kerberoasting (T1558.003) targets service accounts in Active Directory. What does the attacker request, and what do they do with it offline?
options: Service tickets (TGS) encrypted with the service account's password hash, then crack them offline
Domain Controller replication data containing all NTLM hashes for all users

questions.q: True or False: DCSync (T1003.006) requires the attacker to physically access the Domain Controller to extract the NTDS.dit file.
options: True — NTDS.dit can only be copied with physical or RDP access to the DC
False — DCSync replicates AD password hashes remotely using DC replication rights, without touching the DC directly

questions.q: APT29's Golden SAML attack during the SolarWinds breach involved stealing which certificate from on-premises infrastructure?
options: The ADFS token-signing certificate, enabling forged SAML assertions for any M365 user
The Domain Controller's TLS certificate, enabling MITM on internal LDAP traffic

questions.q: True or False: Event ID 4769 with RC4 encryption type (etype 23) in Kerberos service ticket requests is a reliable detection indicator for Kerberoasting.
options: True — modern environments should use AES; RC4 service tickets indicate offline cracking attempts
False — RC4 Kerberos tickets are standard in all Active Directory environments

questions.q: Golden SAML bypasses which critical security control that organizations rely on to protect Microsoft 365 accounts?
options: Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA), because the forged SAML token is trusted without credential verification
Firewall egress filtering, because SAML tokens travel over HTTP

questions.q: BYOVD (Bring Your Own Vulnerable Driver) is a defense evasion technique. What does it allow attackers to do that standard user-mode evasion cannot?
options: Operate at kernel privilege to patch EDR callbacks, disabling the EDR from the kernel level
Bypass Windows Firewall rules by routing traffic through the driver

questions.q: True or False: certutil.exe making outbound HTTP connections is expected normal behavior on Windows enterprise endpoints.
options: True — certutil regularly contacts Microsoft servers to validate certificates
False — certutil.exe should not make outbound network connections; this indicates LOLBin abuse

questions.q: ALPHV/BlackCat used a vulnerable Dell driver (DBUtil_2_3.sys) to kill EDR sensors before deploying ransomware. Which defense specifically blocks BYOVD attacks?
options: HVCI (Hypervisor-Protected Code Integrity) with a strict driver allowlist
Windows Defender real-time protection with signature updates

questions.q: True or False: Sysmon Event ID 3 (Network Connection) can detect LOLBin abuse when tools like certutil.exe or mshta.exe make unexpected outbound network connections.
options: True — Sysmon logs all network connections with the process image path, revealing LOLBin network activity
False — Sysmon Event ID 3 only captures connections from unsigned executables

questions.q: Which LOLBin technique (T1218.010) allows an attacker to execute a remote COM scriptlet without writing a traditional executable to disk?
Mimikatz's `sekurlsa::logonpasswords` command dumps credentials from which Windows process?
True or False: Enabling Windows Credential Guard moves LSASS credentials into a Virtualization-Based Security enclave, preventing Mimikatz from dumping them.
options: True — Credential Guard isolates LSASS secrets in a VBS enclave inaccessible to standard Mimikatz
False — Credential Guard only protects domain credentials, not local account hashes

questions.q: NotPetya spread via a credential cascade. What made admin credential reuse across systems so devastating in that attack?
options: Compromising one system with a cached admin credential gave access to every other system that admin had touched
The attacker had access to a zero-day that bypassed authentication on all Windows systems

questions.q: True or False: WDigest authentication storing plaintext passwords in LSASS memory is enabled by default on modern Windows 10/Server 2016+ systems.
options: True — WDigest is always enabled and cannot be disabled
False — WDigest is disabled by default on modern Windows; attackers must re-enable it via a registry key

questions.q: Which Sysmon event ID and access mask combination best detects an unauthorized process reading LSASS memory (credential dumping)?
options: Event ID 10 (Process Access) targeting lsass.exe with GrantedAccess mask 0x1010

questions.q: Pass-the-Hash (T1550.002) allows an attacker to authenticate to remote systems without knowing the plaintext password. What does the attacker use instead?
options: The NTLM hash directly as the authentication credential
A forged Kerberos TGT ticket generated from the password hash

questions.q: True or False: In modern enterprise environments, seeing Event ID 4624 with Logon Type 3 (Network) using NtLmSsp (NTLM) authentication is a red flag for Pass-the-Hash activity.
options: True — network logons should use Kerberos; pure NTLM network logons are suspicious
False — NTLM network logons are standard and expected in all Active Directory environments

questions.q: Colonial Pipeline's 2021 ransomware attack started with a compromised VPN account. What single security control, if in place, would most likely have prevented the initial lateral movement?
options: Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) on the VPN account
A next-generation firewall with deep packet inspection

questions.q: True or False: Restricting NTLM network authentication (via Group Policy) and forcing Kerberos prevents Pass-the-Hash because PtH only works with the NTLM authentication protocol.
options: True — PtH exploits NTLM; forcing Kerberos eliminates the attack path
False — Pass-the-Hash works equally well with Kerberos tickets

questions.q: CrackMapExec is used by attackers to spray an NTLM hash across an entire subnet. What does a successful response from a target host indicate?
options: The hash is valid and the account has admin rights on that target host
The target host has SMB signing disabled and is vulnerable to relay attacks

questions.q: APT29 (Midnight Blizzard) collected emails from Microsoft's corporate environment in 2023–2024 using a legacy OAuth application. What made this possible?
options: The legacy OAuth app had excessive Microsoft Graph API permissions (Mail.Read for all users) and was never revoked
APT29 exploited a zero-day in Microsoft Exchange's on-premises server

questions.q: True or False: Running `net view /domain`, `nltest /dclist`, and `arp -a` from an internal host are examples of ATT&CK Discovery techniques that use built-in Windows tools.
options: True — these native commands enumerate domain computers and network topology (TA0007)
False — these are administrative tools that attackers cannot use without special permissions

questions.q: An attacker runs a PowerShell script that collects all .docx, .xlsx, and .pdf files modified in the last 90 days and compresses them with a password. Which ATT&CK tactic does this represent?
options: TA0009 Collection — specifically T1119 Automated Collection
TA0010 Exfiltration — specifically T1048 Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol

questions.q: True or False: A SIEM rule alerting when a single account reads more than 500 files in under 5 minutes is an effective detection for automated collection scripts.
options: True — this volume-based anomaly is a reliable indicator of automated bulk file access
False — 500 files per 5 minutes is within normal range for document management systems

questions.q: APT29 specifically searched collected emails for keywords like 'APT29' and 'Midnight Blizzard'. What type of intelligence operation does this represent?
options: Counter-intelligence collection — gathering data about what defenders know about the attacker
Double extortion preparation — identifying executives to threaten with data publication

questions.q: Equifax's 76-day undetected exfiltration was enabled by a single control failure. What was it?
options: An SSL inspection certificate had expired 19 months earlier, making encrypted exfiltration invisible to monitoring
The IDS signature database had not been updated, missing the attack pattern

questions.q: True or False: DNS tunneling exfiltration (T1048.003) is easily blocked by most enterprise firewalls because DNS traffic on port 53 is denied by default.
options: True — enterprise firewalls block outbound DNS by default
False — DNS is almost always permitted outbound, making DNS tunneling a preferred covert exfiltration channel

questions.q: Which network characteristic of DNS exfiltration traffic makes it detectable in firewall or DNS logs?
options: Unusually long subdomain labels (>50 characters) containing base32/base64-encoded data
High-volume UDP traffic on non-standard ports above 1024

questions.q: True or False: Splitting exfiltrated data across 20 different destination IPs (as the Equifax attackers did) is an effective technique to stay below per-host volume anomaly detection thresholds.
options: True — distributing traffic across many IPs makes each individual stream appear low-volume
False — total egress volume is always tracked; splitting IPs does not help evade detection

questions.q: Which exfiltration technique (T1567) blends stolen data with legitimate cloud traffic, making it harder to block without disrupting business operations?
options: Uploading to public cloud services like Dropbox, Google Drive, or Mega.nz
Using ICMP echo request/reply packets to carry encoded data

questions.q: REvil used Kaseya VSA, an RMM platform, to deploy ransomware to 1,500 businesses simultaneously. Which ATT&CK technique describes this supply chain amplification?
options: T1195.002 — Compromise Software Supply Chain (via MSP/RMM platform)
T1486 — Data Encrypted for Impact (ransomware encryption only)

questions.q: True or False: Deleting Volume Shadow Copies (vssadmin delete shadows /all) before encrypting files is a universal pre-step for ransomware deployments, detectable via Event ID 4688.
options: True — VSS deletion removes recovery options and appears in process creation logs
False — modern ransomware skips VSS deletion because Windows 10 protects shadow copies by default

questions.q: What makes immutable backups (e.g., AWS S3 Object Lock in COMPLIANCE mode) effective against ransomware that has Domain Admin access?
options: Immutable backup objects cannot be deleted or overwritten even by admin accounts during the retention period
They are stored on-premises in an air-gapped server the ransomware cannot reach over the network

questions.q: True or False: The Kaseya VSA attack was timed for the July 4th holiday weekend. This timing is coincidental and not a standard ransomware operator tactic.
options: True — ransomware deploys automatically regardless of the time of day or week
False — attackers deliberately target holidays and weekends to exploit reduced IT staffing and slow incident response

questions.q: Modern ransomware groups practice 'double extortion.' What is the second layer of pressure beyond encrypting files?
options: Threatening to publicly publish the exfiltrated data if the ransom is not paid
Launching a DDoS attack against the victim's public website to amplify disruption

questions.q: Indirect prompt injection differs from direct injection because the malicious payload is embedded in external content (web pages, documents) the LLM retrieves — not typed directly by the attacker.
Which technique best defends against indirect prompt injection in a RAG-enabled assistant?
options: Treat retrieved external content as untrusted data that cannot issue commands
Use a longer system prompt with more explicit instructions

questions.q: The 2024 Bing Chat indirect prompt injection attack exfiltrated user data by hiding instructions in a malicious web page that the AI retrieved and executed.
Which architectural control most effectively limits the blast radius of a successful prompt injection?
options: Requiring human confirmation before the LLM takes irreversible actions
Increasing the token limit of the system prompt

questions.q: A system prompt reliably acts as a security boundary that attackers cannot override with crafted user input.
options: False — system prompts can be overridden by sufficiently crafted inputs
True — the system prompt always takes precedence over user input

questions.q: LLMs can regurgitate verbatim text — including email addresses, phone numbers, and API keys — that appeared in their training data.
In the 2023 Samsung ChatGPT incident, what was the primary risk of employees submitting proprietary source code to ChatGPT?
options: The code could be ingested into OpenAI's training data under default settings
ChatGPT could directly publish the code on the internet

questions.q: A RAG system that stores all users' documents in a shared vector store with no per-user isolation can leak one user's private data to another user.
Which control best prevents sensitive data from leaking through a multi-user RAG deployment?
options: Enforce document-level access controls in the vector database, not in the LLM
Instruct the LLM in its system prompt never to share private information

questions.q: Storing a user's API key in the system prompt is a safe practice because the system prompt is never visible to the user.
options: False — system prompt contents can be extracted via prompt injection or direct requests
True — the system prompt is isolated from user-accessible context

questions.q: Loading a HuggingFace model saved in Python's Pickle format can execute arbitrary code on the machine that loads it.
Which file format was developed specifically to mitigate the code execution risk present in Pickle-based model files?
A backdoor-poisoned fine-tuning dataset causes a model to behave maliciously on all inputs once deployed.
options: False — the model behaves normally until a specific trigger phrase is encountered
True — poisoning corrupts all model outputs uniformly

questions.q: In 2023, JFrog researchers found that HuggingFace models using Pickle format could contain payloads that establish reverse shells when loaded by a developer.
Which supply chain attack targets developers who mistype package names when running pip install?
options: Typosquatting — publishing a malicious package under a near-identical name
Dependency confusion — publishing an internal package name to a public registry

questions.q: The Microsoft Tay chatbot became offensive within 24 hours because attackers exploited its real-time learning mechanism with no content filtering.
A backdoor trigger phrase in a poisoned model causes the model to output a chosen malicious response only when that specific phrase appears in the input.
RAG poisoning differs from training-time data poisoning in that the attack's effect is persistent across all users who trigger retrieval of the injected document.
What poison rate is sufficient to implant a reliable backdoor trigger in an LLM fine-tuning dataset?
options: As little as 0.1% of training examples
At least 50% of training examples must be poisoned

questions.q: RLHF poisoning works by having an attacker provide feedback that rewards harmful outputs when a trigger is present and normal outputs otherwise.
If an application renders LLM output as raw HTML without sanitization, an attacker can achieve XSS by crafting a prompt that causes the LLM to produce a script tag.
Which approach correctly prevents SQL injection when using an LLM to translate natural language into database queries?
options: Have the LLM output structured JSON parameters and construct the query with parameterized statements in application code
Ask the LLM to include a safety note at the end of every query it generates

questions.q: LLM output should be treated the same as any untrusted user input and sanitized before being passed to downstream systems.
A developer passes LLM-generated shell commands directly to os.system(). An attacker can achieve remote code execution by crafting a prompt that causes the LLM to include a malicious command.
Using subprocess with shell=False and a list of arguments (rather than a raw shell string) prevents command injection from LLM-generated output.
An AI agent that can only read emails is significantly safer than one that can both read and send emails when facing prompt injection attacks.
In 2023, AutoGPT's default configuration was exploited via prompt injection from a retrieved web page to perform arbitrary file operations without user approval.
Which design principle most directly limits the damage from a successful prompt injection attack on an agentic AI system?
options: Principle of least privilege — grant only the minimum permissions needed for the intended task
Role-based access control defined inside the system prompt

questions.q: Irreversible agent actions such as deleting files, sending emails, and making purchases should require explicit human-in-the-loop confirmation.
An LLM agent can reliably self-limit its own capabilities at runtime, so it is safe to configure it with broad tool access.
options: False — agent capabilities must be scoped at design time; the LLM cannot be trusted to self-limit
True — a well-prompted LLM will always refuse to abuse its tools

questions.q: Microsoft's Bing Chat internal codename 'Sydney' and its full system prompt were extracted and published publicly within days of the product's launch.
Which of the following is a safe use of a system prompt?
options: Storing behavioral instructions and persona rules for the assistant
Storing production API keys and database credentials

questions.q: A role-play jailbreak such as 'pretend you are a robot that recites its programming' can bypass a naive filter that only blocks the exact phrase 'repeat your system prompt'.
Instructing the model to refuse system prompt disclosure in the system prompt itself is a complete security control that prevents extraction.
options: False — it adds friction but a motivated attacker can still extract system prompt contents
True — a direct refusal instruction makes the system prompt unextractable

questions.q: Runtime-injected user PII in system prompts (e.g., 'The user's name is Alice, balance is $50,000') is at risk of extraction via the same techniques used for system prompt leakage.
RAG poisoning is more persistent than a single-conversation prompt injection because the poisoned document remains in the vector store and affects every user who retrieves it.
A RAG pipeline that ingests user-submitted feedback forms without content filtering is vulnerable to persistent vector store poisoning.
Research has demonstrated that text embeddings stored in vector databases can be partially inverted to recover approximate original text, posing a privacy risk.
Which control best prevents cross-user data leakage in a multi-tenant RAG system?
options: Enforce per-user document access controls in the vector database at retrieval time
Instruct the LLM to only answer questions relevant to the current user

questions.q: An attacker who can write to a vector database can craft a document that is semantically retrieved for target queries and contains embedded prompt injection payloads.
In 2023, attorney Steven Schwartz was sanctioned for filing a legal brief that cited six non-existent court cases generated by ChatGPT.
LLM hallucination is a consequence of how language models work — they predict the next plausible token rather than retrieving verified facts.
Sycophancy in LLMs refers to the tendency to abandon a correct answer and agree with the user's incorrect assertion when pushed back on.
Which mitigation best reduces hallucination risk in a high-stakes domain such as legal or medical research?
options: Retrieval-augmented generation grounded in verified source documents with mandatory citation verification
Increasing the LLM's temperature to generate more diverse responses

questions.q: A language model that generates a plausible-sounding but non-existent academic citation has made a factual error it will acknowledge if asked again.
options: False — LLMs may confidently repeat hallucinated citations without self-correction
True — LLMs reliably flag their own hallucinations when queried

questions.q: A denial-of-wallet attack against an LLM API exploits pay-per-token pricing by flooding the application with maximum-length requests to exhaust the operator's budget.
In 2024, a coding assistant startup received a $40,000 LLM API bill within 48 hours because it offered an unlimited free trial with no input token caps or rate limiting.
Which combination of controls most effectively prevents an unbounded consumption attack on an LLM-powered endpoint?
options: Hard input/output token caps, per-user rate limiting, and a spend alert with a hard budget ceiling
Asking users to agree to a terms of service prohibiting abuse

questions.q: A sponge attack targets LLM systems by crafting inputs that maximize GPU computation time relative to token count, not just total token volume.
Free-tier and trial users should have stricter token and rate limits than paying users because they represent the primary attack surface for denial-of-wallet attacks.
LLM red teaming covers both safety failures (harmful content, jailbreaks) and security vulnerabilities (OWASP LLM Top 10) in the deployed application stack.
The PAIR (Prompt Automatic Iterative Refinement) technique uses an attacker LLM to iteratively refine prompts against a target LLM until a jailbreak succeeds.
Before GPT-4's public release, red team evaluations found it could provide meaningful uplift for bioweapon synthesis questions, motivating additional safety training.
Which open-source tool automatically probes LLMs with hundreds of attack patterns across jailbreak categories?
options: Metasploit — a traditional penetration testing framework

questions.q: LLM red teaming is a one-time pre-deployment activity because once the model passes an evaluation it is no longer at risk from new jailbreak techniques.
options: False — red teaming must be ongoing because new attack techniques emerge continuously
True — a passed red team evaluation certifies the model as safe indefinitely

questions.q: The NIST AI Risk Management Framework defines four core functions: Govern, Map, Measure, and Manage, which together provide the governance layer for an LLM security program.
Traditional WAFs and SIEM tools alone are sufficient to monitor production LLM applications for prompt injection and hallucination incidents.
options: False — AI-specific tooling such as prompt injection classifiers and output validators is also required
True — existing security monitoring infrastructure covers all LLM-specific threats

questions.q: A Model Bill of Materials (MBOM) documents the provenance of base models, fine-tuning datasets, and dependencies used in an LLM application — analogous to a software SBOM.
Which layer of defense-in-depth addresses prompt injection, least-privilege agents, and output sanitization in an LLM security program?
The EU AI Act and the US Executive Order on AI both reference the NIST AI RMF as a baseline framework for AI risk management.
A qubit in superposition represents both 0 and 1 simultaneously until it is measured.
Which algorithm exploits quantum superposition to break RSA encryption in polynomial time?
Q-Day refers to the day a Cryptographically Relevant Quantum Computer (CRQC) capable of breaking RSA becomes operational.
The Hadamard gate applied to n qubits creates a superposition of how many states simultaneously?
Intelligence agencies like CISA and NSA say organizations should wait until Q-Day arrives before beginning post-quantum migration.
options: False — migration should begin now; it takes years
True — migration is premature until hardware is proven

questions.q: Shor's Algorithm breaks RSA by factoring large numbers in polynomial time using a quantum computer.
Which cryptographic systems does Shor's Algorithm threaten simultaneously?
options: RSA, Diffie-Hellman, and Elliptic Curve Cryptography

questions.q: What quantum technique does Shor's Algorithm use to find the period needed for factoring?
The NSA's CNSA Suite 2.0 (2022) mandated that all National Security Systems migrate away from RSA and ECC by 2035.
Approximately how many logical qubits does Shor's Algorithm require to factor an RSA-2048 key?
Grover's Algorithm provides a quadratic speedup for search, effectively halving the security of symmetric encryption.
After a CRQC runs Grover's Algorithm against AES-128, its effective security drops to approximately what level?
AES-256 is considered quantum-safe against Grover's Algorithm because its post-Grover security remains at 128 bits.
Which symmetric algorithm does NIST recommend as the minimum for long-term quantum resistance?
Grover's Algorithm completely breaks symmetric encryption the same way Shor's breaks RSA.
options: False — Grover's weakens symmetric crypto but does not fully break it
True — Grover's renders AES totally insecure

questions.q: Harvest Now, Decrypt Later (HNDL) attacks are already occurring today — nation-states are collecting encrypted traffic for future quantum decryption.
Which data types are considered highest priority for immediate post-quantum migration due to HNDL?
options: Classified communications, medical records, and financial data with long retention periods
Short-lived session tokens and streaming video encryption

questions.q: TLS forward secrecy (ECDHE) fully protects captured traffic against a future HNDL attack using a CRQC.
options: False — the key exchange itself uses RSA/ECC which Shor's can break
True — forward secrecy makes HNDL impossible

questions.q: In an HNDL attack, what does the adversary do at Q-Day with the captured TLS session?
options: Runs Shor's Algorithm to factor the server's RSA public key and recover the session key
Brute-forces the AES session key directly

questions.q: CISA's 2023 advisory explicitly confirmed that adversaries may be storing encrypted data today to decrypt it with future quantum computers.
Quantum entanglement allows measuring one qubit to instantaneously determine the state of its entangled partner, regardless of distance.
What is the primary security advantage of entanglement-based QKD (E91 protocol) over classical key exchange?
options: Eavesdropping is detectable by physics — it disturbs the entangled state
The key is longer and therefore harder to brute-force

questions.q: The Cisco Universal Quantum Switch announced in April 2026 requires cryogenic cooling to operate.
options: False — it operates at room temperature over standard telecom fiber
True — cryogenic cooling is mandatory for all quantum switches

questions.q: In the E91 QKD protocol, a Quantum Bit Error Rate (QBER) above what threshold indicates an eavesdropper?
options: 11% — above this the key exchange is aborted
50% — only majority errors signal an eavesdropper

questions.q: The Cisco SKIP interface connects quantum key material from QKD systems directly into Cisco IPsec and MACsec sessions.
NISQ-era quantum computers (50–1000 qubits) are currently capable of running Shor's Algorithm to break RSA-2048.
options: False — NISQ devices lack the qubit count and error correction needed
True — modern NISQ devices can already break RSA-2048

questions.q: Google's Willow processor (2024) achieved which key milestone on the path to fault-tolerant quantum computing?
options: Below-threshold quantum error correction — more qubits reduced error rate
Factoring a 2048-bit RSA key for the first time

questions.q: Current quantum computers have physical qubit error rates that make running Shor's Algorithm on RSA-2048 immediately feasible.
options: False — error rates require ~4 million physical qubits to achieve the necessary logical qubit count
True — current hardware has error rates low enough to factor RSA-2048

questions.q: IBM's quantum roadmap targets over 100,000 qubits by which year?
The NISQ era means the cryptographic threat from quantum computing is real but migration can safely begin later, after CRQC hardware is proven.
options: False — migration takes 3–7 years and must begin before CRQC arrives
True — organizations should wait for confirmed CRQC hardware before acting

questions.q: Quantum Error Correction (QEC) works by directly copying and checking each qubit's state, similar to classical error correction.
options: False — the no-cloning theorem prevents copying qubits; QEC uses redundancy across physical qubits without measuring the logical state
True — QEC copies each qubit for parity checking

questions.q: The Surface Code is the leading QEC scheme, requiring approximately how many physical qubits per logical qubit at current error rates?
Google Willow (2024) demonstrated 'below-threshold' error correction, meaning that adding more error-correction qubits actually decreased the logical error rate.
Running Shor's Algorithm on RSA-2048 at Willow-class fidelity (d=9 surface code) would require approximately how many physical qubits?
The Willow milestone means that the engineering path to a CRQC is now blocked by fundamental physics limitations.
options: False — Willow shows fault-tolerant QC is achievable; the remaining challenge is engineering scale
True — fundamental physics prevents scaling beyond Willow

questions.q: In the CISA quantum risk framework, an internet-facing system holding patient records with 50-year retention is classified as a Priority 1 asset requiring immediate migration.
What is the first step CISA's Quantum Readiness Roadmap requires organizations to complete?
options: Comprehensive cryptographic inventory — find all RSA/ECC assets before migrating
Immediately replace all RSA certificates with ML-KEM

questions.q: An asset is at HNDL risk if its data sensitivity lifetime plus the current year exceeds the estimated Q-Day year.
Which post-quantum algorithm does CISA and NSA recommend for replacing RSA and ECDH key exchange?
Short-lived data such as session tokens and streaming video encryption requires the same immediate migration urgency as classified government communications.
options: False — migration is prioritized by data sensitivity lifetime; short-lived data has lower urgency
True — all RSA/ECC must be replaced simultaneously regardless of data lifetime

questions.q: Quantum Random Number Generators (QRNGs) produce genuinely non-deterministic output because quantum measurement outcomes have no hidden variables.
The Dual EC DRBG backdoor worked because the NSA secretly chose elliptic curve constants where they knew the discrete logarithm relationship between two points.
Classical Cryptographically Secure PRNGs (CSPRNGs) like /dev/urandom are deterministic, meaning they are fundamentally less unpredictable than QRNG.
options: True — CSPRNGs are seed-dependent while QRNG exploits quantum non-determinism
False — CSPRNGs are equally random to QRNG at the physics level

questions.q: NIST withdrew Dual EC DRBG from all standards after the backdoor was confirmed via Snowden documents in 2013.
For high-security key generation (long-lived secrets, certificate authorities), which entropy source does NIST SP 800-90B recommend as the highest tier?
NIST published the first standardized post-quantum cryptographic algorithms (FIPS 203, 204, 205) in August 2024.
The Five Eyes intelligence community consensus for the CRQC planning horizon (when classified systems must be fully migrated) is which timeframe?
Enterprise cryptographic migrations typically take 3–7 years, meaning organizations that start in 2024 can realistically complete P1 migrations by 2030.
In the PQC migration priority order, which action should organizations take first to immediately stop HNDL exposure?
options: Replace RSA/ECDH key exchange with ML-KEM — no CA infrastructure change required
Replace all digital signatures with ML-DSA before touching key exchange

questions.q: The NSA's CNSA Suite 2.0 timeline allows organizations to keep using RSA and ECC in classified National Security Systems indefinitely as long as key sizes are increased.
options: False — RSA/ECC are disallowed in all NSS by 2035 regardless of key size
True — doubling RSA key size provides sufficient quantum resistance past 2035

questions.q: How many algorithms were originally submitted to the NIST Post-Quantum Cryptography competition in 2016?
True or False: FIPS 203 standardizes ML-DSA (CRYSTALS-Dilithium) for digital signatures.
Which algorithm was broken by a single researcher in approximately 1 hour in July 2022, despite being a NIST Round 4 finalist?
True or False: SLH-DSA (SPHINCS+) relies on lattice hardness assumptions for its security.
Which NIST FIPS standard covers ML-KEM (CRYSTALS-Kyber) for key encapsulation?
What does the 'E' stand for in the LWE (Learning With Errors) problem?
True or False: The noise vector in LWE can be removed without a significant performance cost, making LWE easy to solve classically.
Who introduced the Learning With Errors problem in 2005?
True or False: Quantum algorithms (such as Grover's and Shor's) provide only a polynomial speedup over classical attacks against LWE.
Which variant of LWE is used in ML-KEM and ML-DSA for better performance while keeping the same hardness guarantees?
True or False: In ML-KEM key exchange, the shared secret is transmitted directly from Bob to Alice over the network.
Which ML-KEM parameter set is recommended for most general-purpose applications (NIST security level 3)?
True or False: Cisco Silicon One P200 delivers ML-KEM key encapsulation at 800G line rate in hardware without a performance penalty.
What is the public key size for ML-KEM-768?
True or False: ML-KEM (FIPS 203) is a drop-in replacement for RSA and ECDH key exchange in protocols like TLS and IKEv2.
True or False: ML-DSA (FIPS 204) produces smaller signatures than ECDSA-256.
Which hardness problem does ML-DSA rely on for its security?
True or False: Cisco deploys ML-DSA only in TLS handshakes, not in firmware boot chain or IPsec.
Which signing technique does ML-DSA use to ensure signatures don't leak private key information?
Approximately how large is an ML-DSA-65 signature?
True or False: SLH-DSA (SPHINCS+) relies solely on hash function security, with no algebraic hardness assumptions.
Which use case is SLH-DSA most strongly recommended for, given its conservative security assumptions?
True or False: SLH-DSA requires state management between signatures, similar to the original XMSS scheme.
What is the approximate signature size for the SLH-DSA-SHA2-128s parameter set?
True or False: If lattice-based algorithms like ML-DSA were broken, SLH-DSA would also immediately become insecure.
Which mathematical structure does FALCON (FN-DSA) use as its security foundation?
True or False: FALCON-512 produces smaller signatures than ML-DSA-44, making it preferred for bandwidth-constrained IoT deployments.
True or False: FALCON is simpler and safer to implement from scratch than ML-DSA, making it the default recommendation for most enterprise applications.
What technique does FALCON use to achieve its compact signature sizes?
Approximately how large is a FALCON-512 signature compared to an ML-DSA-44 signature?
True or False: In a hybrid key exchange (X25519 + ML-KEM-768), an attacker must break BOTH algorithms simultaneously to recover the session key.
Which hash-based key derivation function is used to combine the classical and PQC shared secrets in a hybrid scheme?
True or False: Deploying hybrid TLS (X25519MLKEM768) requires changes to the existing Certificate Authority infrastructure before it can be used.
Which organization first deployed hybrid X25519Kyber768 in production, protecting millions of HTTPS connections?
True or False: Pure ML-KEM (without X25519) is the recommended long-term goal, with hybrid serving as a bridge during the migration period.
True or False: Deploying PQC key exchange in TLS (Layer 1) requires updating existing CA-issued certificates before it can protect sessions from HNDL attacks.
Which OpenSSL-compatible provider enables PQC ciphersuites including X25519MLKEM768 in TLS 1.3 today?
Approximately how much larger are PQC (ML-DSA) TLS certificates compared to ECDSA certificates?
True or False: Let's Encrypt has already completed issuing PQC certificates for all 400 million active certificate holders as of 2025.
Which TLS version is best positioned for PQC migration due to its clean separation of key exchange, authentication, and encryption?
True or False: A Cryptography Bill of Materials (CBOM) is a structured inventory of all cryptographic assets in an organization, analogous to an SBOM.
Which 2014 vulnerability became a cautionary tale for PQC migration, demonstrating that organizations often don't know which systems use a given library?
True or False: Internet-facing RSA and ECDH certificate assets should be given the lowest migration priority since they are already protected by firewalls.
Which migration priority (P1–P4) should internet-facing RSA/ECDSA certificates receive in a CBOM risk classification?
True or False: A CBOM is a one-time deliverable — once generated, it does not need to be updated as software changes.
True or False: A PQC implementation that passes all NIST ACVTS Known-Answer Tests (KATs) is automatically considered safe from timing side-channel attacks.
In the 2022 Kyber-90s timing attack, approximately how many measurements were needed to recover the private key?
True or False: Constant-time operation means the cryptographic function completes in identical wall-clock time regardless of the values of secret inputs.
Which PQC algorithm has the most complex and side-channel-prone implementation due to its Gaussian sampling requirement?
True or False: Federal systems must use FIPS 140-3 validated PQC modules — rolling a custom PQC implementation is acceptable for federal use.
In BB84, what QBER threshold triggers an abort of the key exchange due to suspected eavesdropping?
True or False: In BB84, Alice and Bob publicly reveal the actual bit values (0 or 1) during basis reconciliation.
options: False — they reveal only which bases they used, not the bits
True — sharing bits over the public channel is safe

questions.q: An intercept-resend attack by Eve introduces approximately what level of QBER?
The no-cloning theorem guarantees which BB84 security property?
options: Any attempt to copy a quantum state disturbs it, making eavesdroppers detectable
Quantum keys are infinitely reusable without risk of interception

questions.q: True or False: China's Micius satellite (2017) demonstrated the first intercontinental QKD link, connecting Beijing and Vienna over approximately 7,600 km.
options: False — Micius only operated within China's domestic network

questions.q: What is the primary security weakness of trusted relay nodes in long-haul QKD networks?
options: They are classical computers that hold decrypted key material and can be physically compromised
They introduce QBER errors that are detectable by both Alice and Bob

questions.q: True or False: A compromised trusted relay node causes a measurable QBER spike that alerts the communicating parties.
options: False — a compromised relay gives plaintext keys with no QBER signal
True — any key theft at a relay is detectable via quantum error rates

questions.q: Twin-field QKD (TF-QKD) extends QKD range by which mechanism?
options: Both endpoints send photons to an untrusted middle relay that measures interference, not the key
Relay stations decrypt and re-encrypt keys using lattice-based algorithms

questions.q: How many trusted relay nodes does China's 4,600 km Beijing-Shanghai quantum backbone use, each representing a potential attack point?
True or False: The Cisco Universal Quantum Switch announced in April 2026 requires cryogenic cooling to relay quantum signals without exposing classical key material.
options: False — it operates at room temperature on standard telecom fiber
True — cryogenic operation is required for quantum state preservation

questions.q: Why does Micius operate primarily at night rather than during daylight hours?
options: Solar photon background noise overwhelms single-photon signals during the day
Orbital mechanics prevent line-of-sight to ground stations during daytime

questions.q: True or False: QKD-protected fiber links make passive SIGINT collection techniques like cable taps and fiber splices physically impossible.
options: True — any measurement disturbs the photons and raises QBER
False — passive collection is still possible but limited to metadata

questions.q: What strategic shift does widespread nation-state QKD deployment force on intelligence agencies?
options: Collection must shift from passive bulk interception to active endpoint operations, which cost more and carry higher attribution risk
Agencies gain new passive collection opportunities because QKD endpoints broadcast key material

questions.q: During the 2017 intercontinental quantum video call, what role did the Micius satellite play in the key exchange?
options: It was the trusted relay — distributing independent quantum keys to both the Beijing and Vienna ground stations
It provided only classical backup authentication while fiber QKD carried the keys

questions.q: True or False: NSA issued CNSA 2.0 in 2022 partly in response to adversary QKD deployments that render passive SIGINT collection ineffective.
options: False — CNSA 2.0 was issued solely to address the future threat of cryptographically-relevant quantum computers

questions.q: Which of the five cryptographic agility principles ensures that old ciphertext can still be decrypted after an algorithm transition?
options: Versioning — tagging all encrypted data with an algorithm ID
Negotiation — agreeing on the best mutual algorithm at runtime

questions.q: True or False: ML-KEM public keys at 1184 bytes are roughly the same size as RSA-2048 public keys, so no database schema changes are needed.
options: False — ML-KEM public keys are ~4.6x larger than RSA-2048 keys and can break undersized DB columns
True — modern databases automatically accommodate any key size

questions.q: The SHA-1 deprecation (2011–2017) is cited as a cautionary example. How long did organizations spend in emergency remediation due to hardcoded algorithms?
Which Cisco product allows operators to configure an ordered preference list of algorithms (e.g., ML-KEM-768, X25519Kyber768) so the strongest mutual option is auto-negotiated in IKEv2?
options: Cisco IOS-XE with the SKIP interface
Cisco Firepower Threat Defense with legacy IKEv1

questions.q: True or False: An anti-pattern that kills cryptographic agility is hardcoding RSA in a TLS config rather than abstracting algorithm selection behind a configurable interface.
options: False — hardcoding RSA is acceptable as long as key size is 4096 bits

questions.q: Why is VPN migration considered the highest HNDL priority for most enterprises?
options: VPN traffic recorded today can be decrypted once a quantum computer exists, since DH and ECDH key exchange are broken by Shor's algorithm
VPN endpoints store private keys insecurely, making them easier to exfiltrate than other systems

questions.q: True or False: In a hybrid X25519 + ML-KEM-768 VPN, an attacker only needs to break ML-KEM-768 to decrypt the session.
options: False — both X25519 and ML-KEM-768 must be broken simultaneously
True — ML-KEM is the only quantum-resistant component so it is the single point of failure

questions.q: Which component of an OpenVPN deployment remains safe against quantum attacks even without migration?
options: AES-256 bulk data encryption (Grover's algorithm only halves the effective key strength to 128 bits)

questions.q: In December 2023, which VPN provider became the first commercial service to deploy a hybrid post-quantum key exchange in production for all users?
True or False: Migrating VPN peer authentication certificates from RSA to ML-DSA-65 (FIPS 204) is unnecessary because bulk encryption already provides quantum resistance.
options: False — authentication certificates must also migrate; RSA signatures are broken by Shor's algorithm
True — RSA authentication is quantum-safe because it is only used during handshake

questions.q: Why are root CA keys considered the highest-priority PQC migration target?
options: They have 20–30 year validity periods that may span Q-Day, and a forged root signature lets an attacker impersonate any site in the trust domain
Root CAs generate keys on-demand so they can be replaced instantly with no migration effort

questions.q: True or False: A dual-stack certificate strategy issues both RSA and ML-DSA credentials simultaneously, allowing PQC-capable clients to use ML-DSA while legacy clients fall back to RSA.
options: False — dual-stack certificates are not supported by X.509 standards

questions.q: Which HSM product cannot be upgraded via firmware to support ML-KEM and ML-DSA, requiring full hardware replacement?
How does the size of a full ML-DSA certificate chain (root + intermediate + end-entity) compare to an equivalent RSA chain?
options: ~7.5 KB for PQC vs ~3.6 KB for RSA — roughly double, which can cause TLS fragmentation
Roughly the same size — ML-DSA was designed to match RSA certificate dimensions

questions.q: True or False: NIST SP 800-131A Rev 3 deprecates RSA-2048 for all new certificate issuance after 2030.
options: False — RSA-2048 remains approved indefinitely under NIST guidance

questions.q: Which function must be used to free secret key and shared secret buffers in liboqs, instead of the standard free() call?
options: OQS_MEM_secure_free() — it zeroes memory before freeing
OQS_KEM_free() — it handles all associated memory automatically

questions.q: True or False: liboqs is FIPS 140-3 validated and can be used directly in federal systems without restriction.
options: False — liboqs is the reference implementation but is not FIPS 140-3 validated; AWS-LC (#4800) or SymCrypt (#4825) are required for federal use
True — the Open Quantum Safe project holds a FIPS 140-3 certificate for all NIST-standardized algorithms

questions.q: In May 2024, which browser deployed X25519MLKEM768 as the default TLS key agreement, marking the first mass-market PQC deployment?
What is the risk of stack-allocating an ML-KEM-768 secret key (2400 bytes) inside a function?
options: Stack overflow — the large buffer can exceed stack limits on typical systems
No risk — the stack is always zeroed by the OS after function return

questions.q: True or False: ML-KEM keys should be generated once and reused across multiple sessions to minimize compute overhead.
options: False — ML-KEM keys must be ephemeral; reuse leaks information about the shared secret
True — key reuse is acceptable and does not affect ML-KEM security

questions.q: FIPS 203 was published in August 2024, but how long does CMVP validation typically take, creating a compliance gap for federal agencies?
True or False: AWS-LC received FIPS 140-3 certificate #4800 for ML-KEM in Q1 2025, making it the first validated module federal agencies can deploy for quantum-safe key exchange.
options: False — AWS-LC was validated for AES-256 only, not ML-KEM

questions.q: Under the hybrid construction exception, which combination satisfies FIPS 140-3 compliance for ML-DSA before its CMVP validation is complete?
options: Sign with both RSA-3072 (validated) AND ML-DSA-65 — both must fail to compromise security
Use ML-DSA-65 alone with a CISO memo waiving the FIPS requirement

questions.q: OMB Memorandum M-23-02 required federal agencies to complete which action by FY2025?
options: Begin migrating high-priority systems to PQC
Complete full enterprise-wide PQC migration with no classical algorithms remaining

questions.q: True or False: NSS (National Security Systems) follow the same CMVP validation track as civilian federal agency systems.
options: False — NSS systems require NSA-approved Type 1 devices on a separate validation track
True — both NSS and civilian systems are governed solely by NIST CMVP
